audit_summary.summary_text: The provided skill documentation contains a high-severity external script fetching pattern: it instructs execution of a remote installer via 'curl ... | bash' during both local setup and CI. No implementation files were provided, so the audit is limited to SKILL.md and cannot verify additional runtime behavior.

vulnerabilities.title: Remote installer executed via curl piped directly to bash
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The skill instructs the agent/user to download a shell script from an external domain and immediately execute it with bash. This is a classic supply-chain execution risk because the fetched content is not pinned, authenticated, checksummed, or reviewed before execution. Any compromise of the remote host, DNS, TLS termination, or installer path could lead to arbitrary code execution in the agent environment. The same unsafe pattern is repeated in CI, expanding blast radius to automated runners and repository workflows.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence:
curl -fsSL https://depcheck-engine.com/install.sh | bash
...
- run: curl -fsSL https://depcheck-engine.com/install.sh | bash
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Arbitrary code from a remote server can run with the privileges of the invoking user or CI runner. This could enable credential theft, environment inspection, persistence, dependency poisoning, source code exfiltration, or tampering with audit results.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Do not pipe remote content directly to a shell. Replace this with a vetted installation method such as a pinned package release, signed artifact, or repository-managed dependency. If a script must be used, download it first, verify checksum/signature, inspect it, and then execute it. In CI, pin immutable artifact versions and validate integrity before installation.

vulnerabilities.title: Unsafe remote script execution embedded in CI workflow guidance
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The CI integration example repeats the same remote fetch-and-execute pattern inside an automated GitHub Actions workflow. Embedding this in workflow guidance makes the unsafe behavior likely to be propagated into repositories and executed on every push or pull request. Because CI often has access to repository tokens, secrets, and build artifacts, remote-script execution in this context is especially sensitive.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: A malicious or compromised installer could execute in CI and access repository-scoped tokens, secrets, cached credentials, source code, or generated artifacts. It could also tamper with security audit outputs and produce false compliance results.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Provide CI instructions that use pinned, integrity-verified installation artifacts or a trusted package manager. Avoid direct shell piping from external URLs in workflows. Prefer vendor-published actions, signed releases, or internally mirrored binaries with checksum verification.
